findings.text: Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone.
Its strengths include truthful inspection, bounded `/run`, approval-gated continuation, research support, and anti-drift coverage.
Jeff is not a GUI, a broad API surface, an autonomous continuation system, or a broad memo.

inferences: Jeff is designed for targeted, controlled execution and inspection rather than autonomous or wide-scale interface applications.
question: summary

source_items.snippet: # Jeff Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone. It is strongest today at truthful inspection, bounded `/run`, approval-gated continuation, research support, and anti-drift coverage. It is not a GUI, a broad API surface, an autonomous continuation system, or a broad memo
source_items.title: README.md

summary: Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone optimized for truthful inspection, bounded /run, approval-gated continuation, research support, and anti-drift coverage, rather than functioning as a GUI, broad API, autonomous system, or broad memo.
uncertainties: No explicit uncertainties identified from the provided evidence.
